items.title: Foreign investment into the US is exploding: US net capital inflows surged to a record +$884 billion in the 12 months ending April 2026. This metric s...
items.description_html:
Foreign investment into the US is exploding:

US net capital inflows surged to a record +$884 billion in the 12 months ending April 2026.

This metric shows how much outside money is entering US financial markets through private investors and official institutions buying US assets.

Net capital inflows have nearly TRIPLED since the start of 2025.

By comparison, the 2021 peak was ~$400 billion, less than half of current levels.

Total private purchases of US equities jumped to $763 billion in April, an all-time high.

Official institutions' purchases rose to a record $121 billion, more than doubling since the start of the year.

Global appetite for US assets is unprecedented.

items.title: US corporate dealmaking is booming: US M&A deal value hit a record $1.89 trillion over the last 4 quarters. This figure has nearly TRIPLED since Q2 20...
items.description_html:
US corporate dealmaking is booming:

US M&A deal value hit a record $1.89 trillion over the last 4 quarters.

This figure has nearly TRIPLED since Q2 2023.

This also exceeds the previous peaks seen in 2021 and during the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble at $1.72 trillion and $1.54 trillion, respectively.

Furthermore, 49% of M&A deal value was financed with stock last quarter, the highest since Q2 2001.

Elevated equity valuations have made stock issuance a relatively cheap financing tool, incentivizing companies to pay with shares rather than cash or debt.

US M&A activity is at record highs.

items.title: Stock market leverage in Asia is beginning to crack: Stock trade defaults reported by securities firms in Taiwan rose to $62 million so far in June, t...
items.description_html:
Stock market leverage in Asia is beginning to crack:

Stock trade defaults reported by securities firms in Taiwan rose to $62 million so far in June, the highest monthly total since data began in 2019.

A stock trade default occurs when investors who bought shares on margin fail to pay for those purchases, or when sellers fail to deliver the shares required to settle the trade.

Trade defaults have soared +300% over the last 2 months and now exceed the 2021 record by ~20%.

This comes as margin purchases have surged +160% over the last 12 months, to $19 billion, close to the all-time high set just before the 2000 Dot-Com crash.

By comparison, margin debt has risen +94% in South Korea over the same period compared to +50% in the final 12 months of the Dot-Com bubble.

Market leverage is out of control.

Recent volatility in tech stocks has raised concerns around OpenAI's IPO.

items.title: Micron is officially the most traded stock in the US. Daily turnover in Micron stock, $MU, surpassed $70 billion for the first time, making it the mos...
items.description_html:
Micron is officially the most traded stock in the US.

Daily turnover in Micron stock, $MU, surpassed $70 billion for the first time, making it the most traded stock in the US.

This metric has surged +1,100% since the start of 2026.

This also puts Micron ahead of both Nvidia, $NVDA, and Tesla, $TSLA, two of the most actively traded US stocks in 2025.

To put this into perspective, neither $NVDA nor $TSLA exceeded $70 billion in daily turnover over the last 12 months.

This comes as $MU has surged +268% year-to-date and has been the biggest contributor to the S&P 500’s +7.5% gain so far this year.

Micron has become the center of the AI trade.

items.title: BREAKING: Iran is pitching a plan where it would charge fees along with Persian Gulf countries for vessels looking to transit the Strait of Hormuz, pe...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Iran is pitching a plan where it would charge fees along with Persian Gulf countries for vessels looking to transit the Strait of Hormuz, per WSJ.

Details include:

1. Iran estimates it could make $40 billion per year by charging for security, safety, and environmental services in Hormuz

2. Iran is pitching the idea to the wider Middle East and as far afield as Beijing

3. "Everyone needs to know that management of the Strait will never return to the way it was before," Iran's Speaker of the Parliament said

4. Iran has already set up an insurance firm it says shippers must use to cross the Strait of Hormuz

Iran appears to be preparing for fees to transit Hormuz after the US-Iran 60-day MoU expires.

items.title: FED OUTCOME – HAWKISH BEGINNINGS ! 10 points. 1. Kevin Warsh’s first FOMC as Chair marked a clear hawkish shift. 2. Fed upgraded it’s inflation out...
items.description_html:
FED OUTCOME – HAWKISH BEGINNINGS !

10 points.

1. Kevin Warsh’s first FOMC as Chair marked a clear hawkish shift.

2. Fed upgraded it’s inflation outlook (core PCE to 3.3%) & removed it’s easing bias

3. The dot plot skewed more restrictive, with 9 participants penciling in a 2026 hike & median no longer showing a 2026 cut

4. Of the 9 participants looking for a hike this year, three look for one hike, five for two hikes, one for three hikes. Only one participant is still looking for a cut

5. The post-meeting statement was trimmed down significantly

6. The forward guidance was dropped entirely, with the only thing close being a reference that: “The Committee will deliver price stability.”

7. Instead of answering any questions about the economy or monetary policy, Warsh announced the creation of five task forces to study various aspects of monetary policy

8. In the press conference, Chair Warsh gave no forward guidance

9. Warsh mostly dwelled on new chair topics, including his five task forces

10. They will deal with: (1) communications (2) the balance sheet (3) data gathering (4) productivity & jobs (5) inflation drivers

#GIFTNIFTY #Nifty #BankNifty #IranWar‌ #Trump #FOMC #Rupee #India @CNBCTV18Live

items.title: RT Nigel D'Souza: Incred note on Aluminium: Bull case on shaky legs -Current valuations of NALCO, Vedanta Aluminium & Hindalco look stretched -Remains...
items.description_html:
RT Nigel D'Souza
Incred note on Aluminium: Bull case on shaky legs
-Current valuations of NALCO, Vedanta Aluminium & Hindalco look stretched
-Remains negative on aluminium names, flagging a potential 30–40% downside.

Key factors:
1️⃣ Primary market shortage may be overstated
• China is nearing its 45 mtpa capacity cap
• China’s secondary aluminium consumption:
2024: 12.7 mt , 2025: 13.35 mt
➡️ Primary deficit is increasingly being replenished by scrap/secondary supply.

2️⃣ Middle East disruptions look temporary, not structural
• ~2.2 mtpa of primary capacity impacted
• Qatar Aluminium & Alba supply can normalize relatively quickly
• EGA’s Al Taweelah remains the key outage risk
⚠️ Aluminium prices could be vulnerable to an ~$800/t correction as the war-risk premium unwinds

@CNBCTV18Live

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Jefferies on Banks RBI FAQs on FCNR-B addresses 2 key issues (1) permitting foreign branch leveraging, which can reduce friction & cou...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Jefferies on Banks
RBI FAQs on FCNR-B addresses 2 key issues
(1) permitting foreign branch leveraging, which can reduce friction & counterparty risks, &
(2) allowing different rates based on tenor & value. While these are supportive to FCNRB mobilisation, as stated in recent report, feel banks are offering lower rates/ IRRs, which can limit mobilisation
Expect FCNR-B rate hikes, esp. for large-ticket deposits for 3yr, & will watch for rate changes by banks

CITI on Banks
RBI’s FAQ dated June 23, 2026 eliminates residual market ambiguity around Swap Window 2.0 & materially widens its scope
Operative shift: Explicit permission to Indian banks — including overseas branches — to extend loans and issue SBLCs against FCNR(B) deposits, & to mark liens thereon.
This single clarification reframes scheme’s character entirely — from a cost-arbitrage instrument into a full-spectrum balance sheet expansion play and is extremely positive for Indian banks.
Layered atop CRR/SLR exemption and favorable LCR treatment, value proposition is multi-dimensional & can potentially attracts higher foreign deposits/borrowings flow than 2013
Scheme’s ultimate efficacy will be determined by execution velocity and leverage depth.
Alpha accrues decisively to institutions that move fastest, leverage deepest, and distribute with broadest global reach
@CNBCTV18Live @_RituSingh

items.title: Don't anchor yourself to one price that if the stock comes here I will look at it , be dynamic and agile to see the facts as they are irrespective of ...
items.description_html:
Don't anchor yourself to one price that if the stock comes here I will look at it , be dynamic and agile to see the facts as they are irrespective of the price , even if the stock corrects to what you like , see the objectivity that can it get worse ,is the reason for the fall what you thought or is it more deep rooted of a problem , having an anchor price in mind wont let you see the facts objectively

Be an opportunist investor , not one that gives in, even if you thought and waited for the stock to come to a certain price , be willing to walk away also if it is not attractive enough , change your opinion when the facts change

Market does not reward your ego or anchor , it rewards disciplined execution at the right valuations and growth adjusted to it , no one variable matters , all of them do hence the need to be agile and not anchored

items.title: Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel @YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice sta...
items.description_html:
Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel

@YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube

I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice stating that my channel, Beat The Street, is under review for a violation of the "Harmful or Dangerous" content policy.

Currently, I have not received a notification pointing to any specific video, description, or timestamp that triggered this flag. Because of this, I am reaching out to request a manual human review of my channel to clear up what I believe is an automated misunderstanding.

Beat The Street is an educational channel dedicated to financial analysis, equity research, and corporate case studies. The content is strictly designed to educate viewers on capital markets and business strategies.

We absolutely do not depict, promote, or encourage any activities causing physical injury, death, or psychological harm. It is highly probable that YouTube’s automated system has misinterpreted standard financial terminology or metaphors used in our video titles and descriptions (such as "market bleeding," "corporate suicide," or "destroying the competition") and flagged them out of context.

I am fully committed to maintaining a safe community on YouTube. If a human reviewer determines that a specific piece of metadata or content is problematic, I am completely willing to edit or remove it immediately.

Could you please look into this case or clarify which specific content triggered the review so I can rectify it?

Thank you for your time, support, and guidance.

Sincerely,
Sudarshan Bhandari

items.title: Max India: Building an Integrated Senior Care Ecosystem in India Max India, through Antara Senior Care, is positioning itself beyond a traditional sen...
items.description_html:
Max India: Building an Integrated Senior Care Ecosystem in India

Max India, through Antara Senior Care, is positioning itself beyond a traditional senior living player.

The company is gradually building an integrated ecosystem around elderly care — combining assisted living, emergency medical support, holistic wellness, insurance, financial literacy, quality standards and clinical governance.

One key initiative is the pilot of an integrated wellness clinic, where modern medicine for emergency care is being combined with Ayurveda, acupressure and other wellness practices.

This matters because senior care is not just about providing residences. As people age, their needs become multi-dimensional — frailty, muscle loss, fall risk, memory care, assisted care, emergency preparedness and emotional wellness all become important.

Max India is also working with government bodies and ministries on senior care advocacy, including submissions to NITI Aayog on insurance and standardisation. This shows that the company is not only building facilities, but also helping shape the broader senior care framework in India.

Another important milestone is Antara becoming the first assisted living provider in India to secure NABH care home accreditation, with 2 care homes now certified.

For Max India, the opportunity is clear:

Senior care = Living + Healthcare + Wellness + Insurance + Standards + Trust.

This integrated approach could help the company differentiate itself in India’s evolving senior care market and build a scalable ecosystem around ageing with dignity. 🏡

items.title: Everybody is talking about Madhu Kela investing in Lloyd Engineering. But It's not a fresh issue. It's a Share Swap Deal where Lloyd bought SISCOL, an...
items.description_html:
Everybody is talking about Madhu Kela investing in Lloyd Engineering.

But It's not a fresh issue.

It's a Share Swap Deal where Lloyd bought SISCOL, and MK Ventures already has stake in SISCOL from the early days.

Story Time: LLOYD X SISCOL DEAL DECODED

To everyone's surprise

DAM Filed for IPO of SISCOL worth 96Crs Fresh Issue in Jul-25 + OFS upto 1.42Cr Equity Shares
Recd Approval in Nov-25 from SEBI

SISCOL is started by Ex-Jindal Steel & Power Guys
Ravikant Uppal and Rajagopal Kannabiran

Madhu along with other investor invested in 2018 when company was formed at face value of Rs. 10
Then Further invested in 2021 at Rs. 65
Post that in 2022 at Rs. 118 per share
And last in 2023 at Rs. 15 per share

Total Shares: 4.06Cr
At Peak Valuation in 2022: 480Crs

Current Equity Valuation: 1220Cr
Lloyd Eng bought 88.12% i.e. 1073Cr

10.82% - Cash paid by Lloyd Eng
41.34% - Share Swap
17.98% - Cash paid by Lloyd Enterprises
17.98% - Cash paid by Streamland Estate

Streamland- Entity of Mastermind Behind Lloyd Group Mr. B. PRABHAKARAN.
Who also hold 9.05% Shareholding in Public via Triveni Earthmovers.
(You heard his name in Lloyd Metal, where he is co-promoter with 17.77% Share along with Mukesh Gupta)

And That ShareSwap Deal of 41.34% include names like
MK Ventures,
Shubkam Ventures,
UAP Advisors(Ameya & Uma Prabhu - Son and Wife of Suresh Prabhu - Union Minister)
Siddarth Pai(Meridian Investment),
N. Jayakumar(Prime Securities),
Surinder Choudhary (Surin Holdings and Krishna Fabrications),
Ranjan Sharma, Poonam Sharma(Star Global) and many more..

Issued 7L more Shares as Pref basis to N Jayakumar's entity - Prime Securities.

Lloyd Engineering targeting 10,000 Cr Rev by FY29-30
FY26 Rev Reported Rev: 1300Crs
Current Order Book: 8,000Cr

One Last Interesting Thing-
Approval of Investment in Lloyds Advance Defence Systems Limited(Defence Arm)
And Borrowings of 1000Cr from Banks.

Last Year company raised 987Cr from Right Issue at Price 32 Rs Per Share.

items.title: Auto Ancillary companies delivered strong Q4FY26 Real theme playing out is content per vehicle expansion Companies are no longer just volume plays The...
items.description_html:
Auto Ancillary companies delivered strong Q4FY26

Real theme playing out is content per vehicle expansion

Companies are no longer just volume plays

They are benefiting from higher lighting content, digital clusters, sunroofs, advanced brakes, premium seating, safety features, EV components, lightweighting & electronic systems

Premiumisation is another important driver

Higher share of SUVs, better lighting, advanced aesthetics, comfort components, suspension systems, sunroofs & safety products are helping companies to outperform the sector

Companies like Lumax Industries, Gabriel, SJS, FIEM, NDR, Pricol & Endurance are direct beneficiaries of this tren

Companies are seeing better RFQs from global OEMs as supply chains diversify

At the same time, margin pressure remains a key monitorable

Most companies highlighted pressure from aluminium, steel, polymers, freight, gas, energy & wage costs

Company wise positioning is clear

Sharda Motor is a lightweighting + emission adjacency + export optionality story.

ASK Automotive is a 2W braking + aluminium lightweighting + aftermarket formalization story.

Sona Comstar is an EV / driveline / railway technology platform.

Gabriel India is a suspension + sunroof + ANAND Group consolidation story.

Lumax Industries is a premium lighting + LED penetration + advanced lighting story.

Belrise is a 2W metal components leader expanding into 4W, CV, proprietary systems & aerospace.

Lumax Auto is a diversified Tier 0.5 system integration story with strong order book & aftermarket momentum.

Endurance is a diversified auto component platform scaling brakes, ABS, suspension, alloy wheels, battery packs & EV electronics.

Sansera is a precision engineering company moving towards aerospace, defence, semiconductors & xEV components.

Pricol is a digital cluster & driver information systems play with P3L & disc brake optionality.

FIEM is a 2W lighting leader with LED & 4W expansion triggers.

SJS is a premium aesthetics & display solutions play with strong exports.

Talbros is a gasket, heat shield, forging & chassis play with export optionality.

NDR is a seating & premiumisation play with new product expansion.

Many companies are investing in new product lines, EV components, advanced lighting, seating systems, aerospace, defence, electronics, railways & aftermarket.

Overall, sector is benefiting from multiple tailwinds - premiumisation, higher content per vehicle, EV opportunity, exports & new product adjacencies.

items.description_html:
We remain very bullish on Manufacturing as a theme

India is still in the early stages of a long manufacturing upcycle

Government support through PLI & import substitution is helping domestic manufacturing scale up

China+1 continues to remain a strong structural driver

Export opportunities are gradually opening up

The best part about this theme is that it can be played through multiple routes

It is not limited to one sector or one category

There are multiple ways to participate in the manufacturing cycle - through industrial capex proxies, building material suppliers, wire & cable players, steel pipes & tube, engineered products, contract manufacturing, auto component suppliers & other manufacturing led businesses

Many stocks have done very well, still the opportunity is huge

items.title: RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde: This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes w...
items.description_html:
RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde
This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes wrote The Economic Consequences of the Peace at this table, looking out over the pond (also notice Duncan Grant’s 1917 portrait of Keynes in the top left corner, painted in the garden).

Keynes liked the area so much that he later leased Tilton House, just down the lane.

As always, Keynes was a dominating figure. Rumor has it that he changed all the clocks in the house to his own eccentric time zone, “Charleston time,” which suited the household’s bohemian lifestyle. Whether this is true is hard to ascertain, but it fits the atmosphere quite well.

It was most interesting for me to be in the room where a book I teach every fall semester came to life. The curator, however, was intrigued by the fact that I seemed to care far more about Keynes than about Duncan Grant or Vanessa Bell. I apologized to her for my professional bias, pointing out that nobody is perfect!

items.description_html:
RT Ankur Bisen
23 people died in the recent Hauz-Raani fire tragedy in Delhi and a cook was arrested and denied bail for the crime.

Modern cities are a post-colonial reality of India and one that India does not have the wherewithal to deal with. This sits at the heart of the issue and what better way to hide it than to arrest the poor cook as the fall-guy, I argue in this piece for @thewire_in

@seemay @mkvenu1

https://thewire.in/urban/hauz-rani-fire-the-cook-is-the-fall-guy

items.title: One downstream consequence of GCC's hiring vs IT GCC's allocate 110 - 130 sqft per person of office space Traditional Indian IT companies typically pa...
items.description_html:
One downstream consequence of GCC's hiring vs IT

GCC's allocate 110 - 130 sqft per person of office space

Traditional Indian IT companies typically pack workspaces tighter at 80 to 110 sq ft per employee

So for same level of hiring , commercial real estate requirement increases by 10% to 20%

Good for the industry!

- Speaks from the shop floor, not a script
- Volunteers hard numbers unprompted
- Says "can't share" plainly — no fudging
- Admits "I don't know" live
- Explains bad numbers , not stories

items.description_html:
INDIA WON’T SIGN US TRADE DEAL WITHOUT COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE: GOYAL

Piyush Goyal said India will not enter into a trade deal with the US until Washington provides appropriate legal backing and tools that give India a comparative advantage over competing economies.
The statement signals India is prioritizing favorable terms over a quick agreement and is prepared to delay the deal if necessary.

EXTREME HEATWAVE SPARKS PANIC BUYING OF FANS & ACs IN FRANCE

The surge in demand is likely to boost near-term sales of cooling appliances and increase electricity consumption.

items.description_html:
DELHI DRAFT EV POLICY PUSHES FOR 95% EV REGISTRATIONS BY 2027

Positive for EV ecosystem; mixed for auto industry

Delhi’s draft EV policy proposes ending new petrol two-wheeler registrations by 2028.

Strong hybrid vehicles may receive road tax concessions.

The policy targets 95% of new vehicle registrations to be electric by 2027.

items.description_html:
Indian cement quietly prefers a BAD monsoon.

Weak rains keep sites running and labour off the farms, so the monsoon quarter doesn't see its usual 25-30% volume drop.

The catch: 60-65% of cement goes into rural housing. Poor rains crush rural incomes, and that demand hit lands over the next 9 months.

items.title: Private labels are a key part of FirstClub's strategy to serve its customer base. There is an interesting contrast in how the larger quick commerce pl...
items.description_html:
Private labels are a key part of FirstClub's strategy to serve its customer base.

There is an interesting contrast in how the larger quick commerce players are approaching this opportunity.

Swiggy Instamart has been the most aggressive, rapidly expanding its private label, Noice, to hundreds of SKUs across categories ranging from dairy to snacks.

Blinkit, on the other hand, has largely limited its private label strategy to staples under the Whole Farm brand and has taken a conscious decision not to aggressively expand beyond that.

Zepto also has a growing portfolio of private labels, including its meat brand, Relish.


Madhav Chanchani: FirstClub has emerged as the most well-funded player in the vertical quick commerce space, raising $86 million in back-to-back funding rounds from investors such as Peak XV Partners and Accel.

But what sets it apart from Blinkit, Swiggy Instamart, and Zepto?

The company is

items.title: FirstClub has emerged as the most well-funded player in the vertical quick commerce space, raising $86 million in back-to-back funding rounds from inv...
items.description_html:
FirstClub has emerged as the most well-funded player in the vertical quick commerce space, raising $86 million in back-to-back funding rounds from investors such as Peak XV Partners and Accel.

But what sets it apart from Blinkit, Swiggy Instamart, and Zepto?

The company is targeting a more premium customer segment - shoppers who are willing to pay more for higher-quality products.

Its average order value (AOV) reflects that strategy, standing at more than twice that of the largest quick commerce players.

More in this deep dive about its game plan - https://www.thearcweb.com/article/slower-quick-commerce-firstclubs-quality-route-to-higher-aov-k7TkblqhQY3Wjzcz

items.title: RT IPO Newsroom: SK HYNIX IS GOING PUBLIC IN THE 🇺🇸 IN JUST 3 WEEKS SK Hynix is planning a $29.4 Billion US listing with trading set to begin on...
items.description_html:
RT IPO Newsroom
SK HYNIX IS GOING PUBLIC IN THE 🇺🇸 IN JUST 3 WEEKS

SK Hynix is planning a $29.4 Billion US listing with trading set to begin on July 10th

This would be one of the top five share sales in history, comparable in size to Saudi Aramco's record 2019 IPO.

SK Hynix controls 57% of the global high-bandwidth memory market by revenue. HBM is the chip that sits inside every NVIDIA GPU powering the AI buildout. Without it, the data centers do not work.

The numbers behind the business:

Q1 2026 operating profit: $24.3 billion, a record high
Q1 2026 sales: $34 billion, nearly tripling year over year
Stock performance: +300% in Seoul this year
Market cap: over $1 trillion

The proceeds will fund additional capacity expansion and purchases of EUV lithography machines needed to make next-generation chips.

Lead underwriters: Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan.

items.title: This is not the first Indian founder WhatsApp has poached, by the way. Those who have tracked the startup ecosystem for a while may remember that What...
items.description_html:
This is not the first Indian founder WhatsApp has poached, by the way.

Those who have tracked the startup ecosystem for a while may remember that WhatsApp recruited Ezetap founder Abhijit Bose as the CEO of WhatsApp India in 2018.

Of course, there was no mega investment in Ezetap.

The startup was eventually acquired by Razorpay in 2022.


Madhav Chanchani: Kunal Shah’s appointment as WhatsApp CEO shows that you can spend two decades building companies in India and still go on to lead a global tech platform.

Now there is a very different path for the ambitious vs Satya Nadella and Sundar Pichai, who built their careers in the US.

Alan Wolff writes that there is no superior alternative to the principles that postwar peak America established: MFN, binding commitments, & national treatment. Abandoning them would mean a trading system based on power & a world of unending uncertainty.
https://www.piie.com/blogs/realtime-economics/2026/international-trade-policy-americas-250th-birthday

items.title: RT Peterson Institute: 📉 The US is driving away international students—that, over ten years, could cost the US as much as the economy of a state t...
items.description_html:
RT Peterson Institute
📉 The US is driving away international students—that, over ten years, could cost the US as much as the economy of a state the size of Wisconsin or Utah.
💡 Since 2025, the US government has restricted international students studying at US universities. Latest available data indicate student visa issuance is about one-third below normal.
💡The decline will affect the high-skill US workforce educated in STEM.
🔬 35% of all STEM workers with a PhD are foreign-born & US-trained.
💼 A sustained one-third reduction in US STEM graduates from abroad would shrink the high-skill STEM workforce by 6.2% overall—& 11.5% at the PhD level.
📉 After a decade, that sustained one-third decline in US STEM graduates from abroad would reduce annual real US GDP by $240-481 billion—as much as the entire economy of South Carolina, Utah, or Wisconsin.
